failure_surface: Code execution failed with SyntaxError due to non-Python content (natural language text and tool markers) being included in execute_code tool parameters, preventing any spreadsheet manipulation from occurring.
root_cause: The agent incorrectly mixed conversational commentary and/or tool-call markers into the code execution cell content. When calling execute_code, only pure executable Python code should be provided without explanatory text preceding or within the code block.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before invoking execute_code, ensure the tool parameter contains ONLY valid, runnable Python code without any prose explanations, status updates, or special characters that could be misinterpreted as code.
evidence: Cell In[1], line 1 with <tool_call> symbol causing SyntaxError in multiple execute_code attempts
Natural language text 'Let me try to check if the solution was created:' appearing inside code execution cell resulting in SyntaxError
The write_file tool successfully wrote valid Python code to /tmp/solution.py, but subsequent execute_code calls contained malformed content
Multiple consecutive SyntaxErrors indicate systematic contamination rather than single typo
No spreadsheet operations were attempted because execution consistently failed before running the script

memory_items.title: Isolate Code from Commentary
memory_items.lesson: When executing code via tools, separate explanatory prose from executable code blocks completely. Never include comments or status updates in the same string passed to execute_code.
memory_items.generalization: Always verify that the execute_code parameter contains syntactically valid code only, with no natural language fragments interspersed.

memory_items.title: Validate Tool Parameters Before Execution
memory_items.lesson: Double-check that tool parameters match expected formats: execute_code expects pure code strings, write_file expects file content only, without mixing purposes.
memory_items.generalization: Review each tool call's output parameter structure against the expected schema to avoid cross-contamination of message types.

memory_items.title: Test Execution Before Full Workflow
memory_items.lesson: Run simple test executions to confirm the execution environment is functioning properly before proceeding with complex multi-step workflows.
memory_items.generalization: A quick print statement or trivial code snippet executed first can reveal environment issues early, saving iteration cycles later.

edits.content:
## Tool Usage Hygiene
- **Execute Code Separation**: The execute_code tool must receive ONLY valid Python code. Do not prepend or interleave natural language explanations, status messages, or tool markers within the code parameter.
- **Parameter Validation**: Before invoking any tool, verify its parameters conform strictly to the expected schema. Mixed-content parameters cause silent or cryptic failures.
- **Environment Verification**: Run minimal test executions first to confirm the runtime environment accepts code correctly before deploying complex logic pipelines.
